data.title: Weekly Crypto Regulation Roundup: Political Pressure, Market Structure Delays, and a Surveillance Flashpoint
data.description:
The past week in crypto regulation exposed a deeper truth about the current policy environment: digital asset oversight is no longer just a technical debate about market structure but a proxy battleground for institutional independence, surveillance power and political leverage.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/weekly-crypto-regulation-roundup-political-pressure-market-structure-delays-and-a-surveillance-flashpoint/

data.title: Trump Praises Economic Advisor Hassett's Communication Skills
data.description: According to Odaily, U.S. President Donald Trump has expressed admiration for Kevin Hassett, the Director of the White House National Economic Council. Trump highlighted Hassett's effective communication skills, contrasting them with the more reserved nature of Federal Reserve officials. He noted Hassett's strong television presence and expressed a desire for him to continue in his current role. The situation remains to be seen.

data.title: Coinbase will release its full-year 2025 financial results report on February 12.
data.description: Coinbase Global announced that it will release its fourth quarter and full-year 2025 financial results after the US stock market closes on Thursday, February 12, 2026, and will hold a webcast at 2:30 p.m. Pacific Time on the same day to discuss its financial results. (Financial Times)

data.title: Trump praised White House National Economic Council Director Hassett
data.description: Trump praised Hassett, director of the White House National Economic Council, saying that Fed officials don't talk much, but Hassett is very articulate. He added that Hassett performs well on television and he wants him to remain in his position; we'll wait and see. (Jinshi)

data.title: The "1011 Insider Whale" increased its Ethereum long positions by 20,000 ETH, bringing its total holdings to $736 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, the "whale that opened short positions after the 1011 flash crash" has increased its ETH long positions again after a month. In the past 5 minutes, it added 20,000 ETH, bringing its single-coin holdings to $736 million (223,340 ETH), with a floating profit of $32.46 million and an overall floating profit of $43.19 million.

data.title: The gold-silver ratio fell below 50 for the first time in 14 years.
data.description: Following the surge in silver prices in the fourth quarter of last year that continued into the new year, the gold-silver ratio, closely watched by precious metals traders, briefly fell below 50 this week for the first time in 14 years. As Goldman Sachs precious metals trader Augustin Magnien pointed out, silver is currently at the heart of trade tensions. Previously, the United States added silver to its list of critical minerals, while China implemented stricter export controls on silver. These geopolitical developments spurred a surge in investor interest, pushing silver prices to record levels, driven by expectations of a Federal Reserve rate cut and a trend towards portfolio diversification. (Cailian Press)

data.title: Grayscale transferred approximately 148 BTC, worth $14.15 million, to a Coinbase Prime address.
data.description: According to Arkham's monitoring, about half an hour ago, Grayscale transferred 148.372 BTC, worth $14.15 million, to a Coinbase Prime address through its Bitcoin exchange-traded fund, Grayscale Bitcoin Trust.

data.title: LSEG Launches 24/7 Blockchain Settlement
data.description:

data.title: Tesla drowning in 8,313 self-driving records as deadline extended
data.description:
Federal safety officials have given Tesla an extra five weeks to respond to questions about its cars breaking traffic rules while using the company’s automated driving system. The electric carmaker asked for the delay earlier this week from the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration. The agency launched its look into what Tesla calls Full Self-Driving, […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/tesla-drowning-in-8313-self-driving-records/

data.title: Polygon Reportedly Slashes 30% of Staff After Massive $250M Payments Bet
data.description:
Polygon Labs reportedly slashed 30% of its workforce following its $250 million acquisitions, including Coinme and Sequence, as it pivots to stablecoin payments and consolidates teams under its Open Money Stack strategy.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/polygon-layoffs-30-percent-staff-250m-payments/

data.title: Jefferies’ Wood Ditches Bitcoin, Warning Quantum Computing Could Break It
data.description:
Jefferies' Christopher Wood eliminated a 10% Bitcoin allocation in favor of gold, warning that quantum computing advances could undermine cryptographic security as researchers estimate 30% of circulating supply remains vulnerable to future attacks.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/jefferies-wood-ditches-bitcoin-warning-quantum-computing-could-break-it/

data.title: Address Poisoning Attack Results in Significant USDT Loss
data.description: According to Foresight News, Cyvers Alerts has reported an address poisoning attack resulting in the loss of 509,000 USDT. The victim intended to transfer funds to the address 0xe842....D3E6F but mistakenly sent them to the attacker's address 0xe842....f3e6F. Previously, the victim had already transferred 5,000 USDT to the attacker's address.

data.title: Meme Activity Surges to 50% of Solana DEX Weekly Trading Volume
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, data from Blockworks Research indicates that meme activity has returned to account for 50% of the weekly trading volume on Solana's decentralized exchanges (DEX) after a six-month hiatus. Notably, the proportion of meme activity in Solana DEX trading volume has seen positive growth for three consecutive weeks.

data.title: Galaxy Research Director: Stablecoin yield provisions become a "deadlock" in crypto structure legislation; Senate hearings to resume as early as late January.
data.description: Galaxy Research Director Alex Thorn stated in an article on the X platform that although Senate Banking Committee Chairman Tim Scott has not yet announced a new hearing date, the earliest the committee could reconvene for the revision hearings is the week of January 26-30, due to the Senate recess next week. The Senate Agriculture Committee, which handles CFTC-related matters, had previously postponed its revision hearings to January 27. Tim Scott had previously announced a postponement of the Crypto Markets Structure Act hearings. It is understood that stablecoin yields are a key sticking point in the negotiations. Bank lobbying groups are actively pushing for restrictions on stablecoin rewards, fearing that interest-bearing stablecoins could siphon off bank deposits and destabilize the banking system. Compromises proposed to gain congressional support were ultimately deemed unacceptable by the stablecoin industry, with some believing the issue is a matter of survival. Other unresolved issues include restrictions on DeFi and illicit activities, as well as restrictions on tokenized security innovation.
